title: SEM General Fixation
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"5mhe4","text":"Fixative:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":9}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6t49b","text":"One (1) ml. 25% Glutaraldehyde was mixed with 3.2 ml. GD (glass distilled) H20 – MilliQ H2O is fine. This gives you 4.2 ml. of 6% Glutaraldehyde.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"a0ql1","text":"Because it is in a 10mL ampule – 10mL Glut + 32 mL H2O","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dipgk","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3crve","text":"Mix the 6% Glutaraldehyde and the 0.2M buffer together in a 1:1 mix.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3t6gd","text":"This gives 3% Glutaraldehyde in 0.1M.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"f1u2j","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"epaeg","text":"The volume of the buffer should be 2-3X volume = follow the suggested volumes at each fixation step.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dda03","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2tfqd","text":"To order anhydrous ethanol (200 proof), Call Chem Stores (4-3081) to get the most up to date cost of 1 pint. Send Bridgit in the division of biology accounting office an e-mail saying you want ethanol from chem store = bcarpen1@utk.edu. Give her the lab account number and how many pints we want and the cost per pint. She will send a transfer order to your e-mail. You can pick up the ethanol from the bottom of the chemistry building.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":219,"length":16}],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"","data":{"guid":"FF83DBF03BF511EBA3F97D5010A9EA8E","url":"http://bcarpen1@utk.edu"}}}}
steps: Grow 50mL cells to appropriate OD600 and wash in 1X PBS. Pour of supernatant.
Primary Fixation: 3% Gultaraldehyde in 0.1M cacodylate (1X PBS)* = add 500uL and vortex briefly Fix for 1 hour at room temperature. *Any buffer should be fine = use 1X PBS
Rinse the sample in a buffer (1X PBS) at least 3 times for 10 minutes each.
Secondary Fixation: (Not usually required) 2% Osmium tetroxide in 0.1M cacodylate (1X PBS) = add 200uL Fix for 1 hour at room temperature.

Rinse the sample in the water at least 3 times for 10 minutes each.** **If working with single cells such as bacteria, allow the sample to settle onto a 3X4mm silicon chip after the final water wash. The Si chip (clean off with Methanol – cells go on SHINY side) is then processed through the remaining dehydration steps
Put the samples at 4 o C overnight in 500uL 1X PBS
Spin down before heading to JIAM. – Don’t resuspend
Bring samples to JIAM – SEM facility
Clean the silicon chip with methanol
Poly-L-lysine – add 20uL to the chip and let sit for 2 minutes – blow the liquid off with compressed air
Add 20uL of cells to the silicone chip and let sit for 25 minutes.

Dehydrate sample through a graded ethanol series 25% Ethanol 10 min. 50% Ethanol 10 min. 70% Ethanol 10 min. 95% Ethanol 10 min. 100% Ethanol 10 min 100% Dry Ethanol 10 min – Freshly opened container of anhydrous ethanol The purpose of 2-100% Ethanol steps is to account for any water that transfers from 95%.
Critical point dry – Follow the protocol
creator: Eadewunm
